summary: Frontend-only regression for major UI restructure (Kabinet Samudera → HIMTIKA-style). All 50 critical assertions passed across home page sections, hero carousel auto-rotation, counter animations, FAQ accordion, navbar dropdowns (5 dropdowns with correct item counts), 9 new pages (all 200 + correct data-testids), 4-column footer, and mobile responsive nav. Theme toggle correctly removed. No regressions. Backend skipped per request (verified 21/21 in iteration_1).
action_items: (carryover from iteration_1, not retested) Brute-force lockout uses request.client.host which is the cluster proxy IP behind ingress — lockout never triggers in production. Use X-Forwarded-For or key on email alone.
(carryover from iteration_1) /api/auth/me returns 401 on every public page load for guests — handle 401 silently in AuthContext to suppress 20+ console errors per page load.
critical_code_review_comments: HeroCarousel auto-rotation uses 4500ms interval — matches spec. Slides 2 and 3 use external Unsplash URLs; if those go down hero will look broken — consider self-hosted fallback assets.

Navbar dropdown opens on click only (good UX-wise), closes on outside click via mousedown listener. ChevronDown rotation correct. mousedown vs click can fire before button onClick toggles state — currently works, but consider using pointerdown for consistency.
FaqAccordion does not set aria-expanded on the trigger button — minor a11y gap. Recommend adding aria-expanded={open} and aria-controls to the <button> for screen-reader users.
Home.jsx uses Promise.all for berita+program-kerja+anggota; failure of any silently swallowed via empty catch — at least log to console.error for observability.
AuthContext fires GET /api/auth/me on every public page mount even when no cookie exists, producing 20+ noisy 401 console errors per page (carryover).
Footer.jsx COLS array hard-codes link strings — fine for now, but if more pages/links are added consider centralizing nav config so Navbar + Footer share one source of truth.
Hero slide images use w-full h-full object-cover (good), but inline styles for borderRadius could be moved to CSS class for consistency.
CountUp uses IntersectionObserver triggered animation — confirmed working (counter-program-kerja shows '8+' after scroll-into-view).
test_credentials: Admin: himaproditrpl@gmail.com / Samudera@2026 (not used in this iteration — public-only testing).
seed_data_creation: No new seed data; relied on existing backend seeds (program-kerja=9 items shown on /kalender, project page renders 6 cards).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend UI restructure verified end-to-end. All data-testids match request: top-marquee, navbar, hero-section, hero-indicators, logo-bar, about-section, counters (counter-program-kerja/event/pengurus), kenali-section, events-section, blog-section, faq-section, footer (footer-col-media/tentang-kami/events/aplikasi-publik), nav-{slug}-btn/menu, sejarah/logo/awards/seminar-workshop/bootcamp/diesnatalis/kalender/info-lomba/project pages. Hero rotates every 4.5s through 3 slides, height=100vh, border-bottom-radius=48px. FAQ uses openIdx state (default 0, first item open) and toggles on click — does NOT use aria-expanded so test that attribute against text-length change instead. Navbar dropdowns use data-testid pattern: nav-{label-kebab}-btn and nav-{label-kebab}-menu. Mobile hamburger at [data-testid=navbar-mobile-toggle] reveals [data-testid=navbar-mobile-menu] with all 4 dropdown groups as headers + 19 sub-links.
rca of the issue: No new bugs found in this iteration. Iteration_1 carryover RCA still valid for brute-force-lockout and /api/auth/me console noise.
